title: ParseError lacks #[non_exhaustive], is a semver trap
description: ParseError is in pub mod parse_error with all-pub fields and NO #[non_exhaustive] annotation. Any future addition of a field (e.g. a byte offset, a source snippet) is a semver breaking change because callers can construct and pattern-match ParseError directly. SieveError has #[non_exhaustive] on both struct and enum; ParseError should too. Additionally, ParseError is not re-exported at the crate root — callers must write sieve_form::parse_error::ParseError rather than sieve_form::ParseError — which is inconsistent with SieveError/SieveErrorKind.
